---
title: "Scaffolding AI Interaction"
subtitle: "A Low-Cost Intervention for Equitable Student Engagement"
format:
  html: default
format-links: false
---

## Project Overview

Australian universities are embedding AI tools in assessment, but the skills required to use those tools effectively are not equally distributed. Students from low socioeconomic backgrounds and those who are first in their family to attend university have fewer informal support networks to develop productive AI interaction habits before they arrive. Without structured scaffolding, this access gap becomes a participation and attainment gap.

This project implements and evaluates a low-cost, scalable intervention: a **conversational nudge** appended to AI responses during structured lab sessions. The nudge makes the conversational path structurally easier, reducing the risk of uncritical acceptance at the point where it matters most. The equity analysis asks whether the benefit is uniform or differential - whether students who had fewer prior scaffolding opportunities gain more from the intervention.

The intervention is grounded in the [Conversation, Not Delegation](https://michael-borck.github.io/conversation-not-delegation/) framework (Borck, 2026): the quality of AI-assisted work depends on whether the student is directing the AI through active conversation or passively accepting its output.

**Funding:** Curtin NBF Learning & Teaching Grant 2026 - AUD $20,000

## Project Team

| Role | Name | Discipline | Unit |
|------|------|-----------|------|
| Lead | Michael Borck | Information Systems | ISYS6018 Information Security Audit and Control (~50 students) |
| Co-I | Marcela Moraes | Marketing | MKTG1000 Discovering Marketing (~220 students) |
| Co-I | Torsten Reiners | Supply Chain Management | MGMT6076 Supply Chain Digitalisation and Innovation (~30 students) |
| Co-I | Renee Ralph | People and Culture | MGMT3014 Strategic Rewards and Performance Management (~800-1000 students) |
| RA | To be recruited | - | - |

## Timeline

| Period | Phase | Status |
|--------|-------|--------|
| **March-April 2026** | Foundation: HREC, confirm co-Is, configure interface | **Current** |
| May-June 2026 | Instrument development, pilot, power analysis | |
| July-Oct 2026 | Data collection: lab sessions across 3+ units (Semester 2) | |
| Nov-Dec 2026 | Coding, reliability checks, analysis | |
| Jan-Mar 2027 | Paper draft, resource pack | |
| Apr-Jun 2027 | Reporting, scope follow-on study | |

## Quick Links

**Study Design**

- [Methodology](methodology.qmd) - research design, measures, analysis plan
- [Paper Outline](paper-outline.qmd) - ACIS 2027 target structure
- [Literature Review](literature-review.qmd) - thematic review, working document

**Documents**

- [Grant Application](grant/nbf-grant-application.qmd) - NBF grant (v6.0)
- [Ethics Package](ethics/index.qmd) - HREC application and supporting documents
- [Research Instruments](instruments/index.qmd) - coding scheme, survey, task briefs, nudge variants

**Tools**

- [Chat Interface](https://chat.locolabo.org/session){target="_blank"} - lab session AI chat tool
- [keep-asking](https://github.com/michael-borck/keep-asking){target="_blank"} - research programme
- [keep-asking-app](https://github.com/michael-borck/keep-asking-app){target="_blank"} - chat tool source

::: {.callout-warning}
## Items requiring confirmation before submission

- [x] Co-investigator units and contributions (Marcela, Torsten, Renee)
- [ ] HREC submission date
- [ ] Equity cohort percentages from Curtin SIMS (% low SES, % first-in-family)
- [ ] Power analysis (pending cohort sizes)
- [ ] ACIS 2027 deadline and location
- [x] Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander section - removed
- [ ] Talk to Business and Law RDA (Dr Joanne McEwan)
:::
